title: Bloom Energy Q1 2026 Earnings Report and Forecast
text: Bloom Energy's Q1 2026 earnings report revealed substantial beat versus consensus expectations and raised full-year guidance reflecting strong execution and market tailwinds. Revenue of $751.05 million exceeded analyst estimates and delivered 130.4% year-over-year growth. Non-GAAP EPS of $0.44 substantially exceeded consensus estimate of $0.10, representing 340% beat. Product revenue of $653.3 million demonstrated accelerating AI data center adoption. Gross margin expansion to 31.5% from prior 28.7% demonstrates operational leverage from volume scaling. Operating income surge to $129.7 million reflects improving profitability trajectory. The company raised 2026 revenue guidance to $3.4-$3.8 billion, representing approximately 80% year-over-year growth at midpoint. Non-GAAP EPS guidance of $1.85-$2.25 suggests continuation of strong profitability. Non-GAAP gross margin guidance of 34% indicates further margin expansion ahead. Operating income guidance of $600-$750 million reflects confidence in execution. The $20 billion backlog provides visibility into multi-year revenue. Management's commentary emphasized scalability of manufacturing process and ability to rapidly convert backlog to revenue. Multiple analyst upgrades followed earnings, with consensus price target approximately $240-$260 range.
